question_text: Explain why a straw appears bent when placed in a glass of water, even though the straw is actually straight.
correct_answer: Light from the straw refracts (bends) as it passes from the water to the air. This bending of light rays makes the part of the straw in the water appear to be in a different position than where it actually is, causing it to look bent.
explanation: When light travels from a denser medium (water) to a less dense medium (air), it bends away from the normal. This refraction causes our eyes to perceive the straw's image as if it were bent at the water's surface, even though the physical straw remains straight.
